title: Uncial — the insular manuscript hand
chartDefinition: calligraphyExemplar — uncial lower-case, nib angle 20°, 4 nib-widths x-height, letters a, d, g, m, n shown with ductus
sources: Edward Johnston, Writing and Illuminating and Lettering (1906) — chapter on round-hand alphabets and their historical forms
notes: The round, Roman-influenced hand used in Insular manuscripts. Low nib angle (10–20°), generous rounded strokes, minimal ascenders and descenders. Distinct from half-uncial (which has more pronounced ascenders). Teaches the letterform family: broad arch letters (n, m, h), round letters (o, c, e), and the characteristic uncial forms of a, d, g.
